finding: Stage 12.5 is the strongest current false-track filter
interpretation: keeps mean 0.978 true tracks while removing nearly all false tracks
clean-truth calibration under-retains noisy true tracks; track-purity fixes it
MLP and GRU are close and strong; TCN retains fewer true tracks

finding: High thresholds (9/12 dB) carry a windowability denominator caveat
interpretation: few/no windowable false tracks remain, so cross-method false-reduction is not apples-to-apples there

finding: Diffusion helps regularization/gap filling but is not the primary filter
interpretation: stage-15 diffusion smooths tracks and fills gaps but does not beat stage 12.5 as a classifier

finding: Robustness currently limited to available days
interpretation: single-day (2022-06-06) evidence; multi-day confirmation still required
